insights:
DeepSeek V4 Flash is the single best deal among currently available models. It delivers an intelligence score of 40 out of 60, a blended price of $0.06 per million tokens, and a value score of 666.7—the highest in the dataset—while maintaining 109 tokens per second throughput and a 1,000K context window. This combination of solid capability, rock-bottom cost, and competitive speed makes it the clear choice for developers who want maximum intelligence per dollar without sacrificing context length or latency.

Two notable runners-up offer different trade-offs. MiMo-V2.5, at the same $0.06 price point, scores a slightly lower 37 intelligence but brings a strong arena code ELO of 1430, making it a viable alternative for coding tasks; however, its 78 t/s speed is noticeably slower. On the higher-intelligence end, MiniMax M3 achieves a 44 intelligence score and a code ELO of 1495—the best in the list—but costs $0.22 per million tokens, yielding a lower value score of 200. DeepSeek V4 Pro also offers 44 intelligence for $0.18 per million tokens but at only 62 t/s and a value score of 244.4, so it is less cost-efficient than the Flash variant.

For the most common use case—cost-sensitive API calls where budget constrains throughput—the recommendation is clear: default to DeepSeek V4 Flash. It provides the highest value score by a wide margin while still delivering adequate intelligence for many production tasks, including summarization, classification, and structured output. If your application demands higher reasoning or code generation quality and you can accept a tenfold increase in cost per token, MiniMax M3 is the strongest premium option, but for almost all cost-conscious deployments, DeepSeek V4 Flash is the optimal starting point.
